compiled_state.title: worktree-tracking-comment contract doc says :complete is posted before merge but harvest-worktree.sh posts it after merge in cleanup trap
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: The worktree-tracking-comment.md contract specifies :complete is 'Posted after all implementation work is done and committed in the worktree, before merge back to the session branch.' However, harvest-worktree.sh posts WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete in _harvest_cleanup() which fires after the merge. For the success path, the timing differs from the stated contract. Either update the contract to say 'after merge' or move the success-path comment write to before the merge step. (Minor semantic mismatch found during code review of 16f8-4b57.)

comments.body: Investigation: The contract doc and code are already aligned. Line 27 of worktree-tracking-comment.md reads 'Posted by harvest-worktree.sh in its cleanup trap after the merge attempt (success or failure).' This matches harvest-worktree.sh _harvest_cleanup() at lines 82-103 which posts in the ERR/EXIT trap with outcome=merged (exit 0) or outcome=discarded (non-zero). Both were introduced together in commit 19523fb4. The ticket description quotes an earlier draft ('before merge back to the session branch') that never made it into the committed doc. No code or doc change needed — leaving ticket open per Rule #14 (no autonomous close without a code change). Recommend user close as invalid / already-resolved.
comments.author: Test
